title: Rustic Argentinian Beef Wrap
description: A tangy, chewy wrap that blends classic Argentinian flavors with a light, pescatarian twist, featuring corned beef or salmon folded into warm tortillas.

ingredients.name: corned beef
ingredients.notes: replace with corned salmon for pescatarian version

instructions: Preheat a large skillet over medium heat and lightly coat with a teaspoon of olive oil.
Season the corned beef (or salmon) with salt and pepper and place it in the skillet, searing until browned and caramelized, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced onions and diced tomatoes to the skillet, cooking until the onions soften and the tomatoes start to break down, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the shredded cabbage and cook until it wilts slightly, about 2 minutes.
Warm the tortillas in a dry pan or microwave until pliable, about 30 seconds each.
Lay a tortilla flat, spread a thin layer of the cooked meat and vegetable mixture, and sprinkle with fresh cilantro.
Fold the sides over the filling and roll tightly to form a wrap, sealing the edges with a dab of tomato sauce or a small amount of water if needed.
Serve the wrap warm with lime wedges on the side for squeezing over the top.
Serve the wrap warm with lime wedges on the side for squeezing over the top.
